content.problemStatement:
Om has a string SSS consisting of characters ‚àó*‚àó, 444, and 000 only.


The character ‚àó*‚àó can be replaced by either 444 or 000.
Om wants to count the total number of subsequences of 404404404 present in all the possible strings generated after replacing ‚àó*‚àó.

As the number can be huge, you must output the number modulo 109+710^{9}+7109+7.

For example, if the given string is S=S =S= 4‚àó4‚àó4*4*4‚àó4‚àó, the possible strings after replacing ‚àó*‚àó are:


404040404040: Only one subsequence of 404404404 is present, that is S1S2S3S_1S_2S_3S1‚ÄãS2‚ÄãS3‚Äã.
404440444044: Two subsequences of 404404404 are present, that are S1S2S3S_1S_2S_3S1‚ÄãS2‚ÄãS3‚Äã and S1S2S4S_1S_2S_4S1‚ÄãS2‚ÄãS4‚Äã.
444044404440: No subsequence of 404404404 is present.
444444444444: No subsequence of 404404404 is present.
Thus, total number of 404404404 subsequences present in all possible generated strings is 333.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî size of the given string.
The second line has a string SSS of size NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output the total number of subsequences of 404404404 present in all the possible strings generated after replacing ‚àó*‚àó.

As the number can be huge, you must output the number modulo 109+710^{9}+7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^{6}106.

440444044404: Two subsequences of 404404404 are present, that are S1S3S4S_1S_3S_4S1‚ÄãS3‚ÄãS4‚Äã and S2S3S4S_2S_3S_4S2‚ÄãS3‚ÄãS4‚Äã.

Thus, the total number of 404404404 subsequences present in all possible generated strings is 444.
Test case 222: Already explained in problem statement.
